releases.title: Catalog replacement correction
releases.summary: This maintenance release corrects how chained question replacements are resolved while preserving all player history and earned points.

highlights.text: Corrected six superseded questions that could remain available after a multi-step replacement.
Preserved existing attempts, points, mastery, feedback, approvals, and question history.

items.text: Chained replacements now resolve to the durable original question even when an intermediate replacement was never published to a particular environment.
The correction retires only the six superseded active questions; it does not delete or rewrite attempts, scores, mastery, feedback, editorial decisions, or immutable versions.
Historical topic and subtopic progress remains attached to the retired question, while its replacement begins with full value as a new question.

releases.title: A stronger, fully reviewed core
releases.summary: This release completes Mindspan's first full editorial review of the 900-question core catalog, substantially improves question quality and variety, makes answer matching more natural, and provides clearer ways to understand individual and group progress.

highlights.text: Completed editorial review of all 900 questions in the core catalog.
Improved 438 questions for accuracy, variety, difficulty, explanations, and accepted answers.
Numeric answers and familiar surnames are now accepted more naturally.
Achievement progress and group knowledge coverage are clearer.
Improved sign-in reliability and question-review tools.

items.text: Every current question in the eight starter packs, Easy Does It, and Trivia 101 has received editorial review.
Revised 438 questions based on player feedback, systematic pack reviews, and duplicate analysis.
Rebuilt substantial portions of the Geography, History, Music, Sports, and Science & Nature starter packs to provide better variety and more useful knowledge.
Reduced repetitive battle-location, exact-year, season-count, obscure element, constellation-abbreviation, athlete, venue, and model-number questions.
Strengthened explanations, additional context, distractors, difficulty ratings, classifications, sources, and accepted answers.
Replaced all questions rejected during final review while preserving previous attempts and earned points.
Removed confirmed duplicate questions while retaining genuinely distinct questions that happen to share an answer.
Numeric answers now accept equivalent written forms, such as 4 and four.
Familiar surnames are accepted when a well-known person's full name is the answer.
Leading articles such as a, an, and the continue to be handled automatically without requiring redundant aliases.
Accepted-answer aliases were expanded where player and reviewer feedback identified reasonable responses.
Achievement entries now show live progress toward unfinished milestones.
Completed achievements are ordered with the most recently earned first.
Group Activity entries now include details about the achievement that was earned.
Group coverage now uses consistent percentage-based heatmaps and summarizes strengths, specialties, and knowledge gaps.
The landing and login pages now recognize an existing authenticated session and continue directly into Mindspan.
Question-review statistics failures no longer make the entire reviewer page unusable.
Editorial approvals now follow exact unchanged question content safely between development and production.
Each question now has one primary subtopic plus optional reviewer-only detail tags.
The Question Index provides pack-scoped statistics and direct access to individual reviews.
Previous and Next controls remain visible while reviewing a pack.
Reviewers can see submitted player answers and aggregate answer counts.
Reviewers can award missed credit when player feedback identifies a valid answer that should have been accepted.
Rejected questions are now included in Question Quality action-item exports.
Eight additional 50-question expansion packs have been prepared and remain disabled while awaiting review.

releases.title: Faster play, better questions
releases.summary: This update makes the play loop more responsive, substantially improves the question catalog, and introduces better tools for tracking and reviewing question quality.

highlights.text: Answers are checked faster, and the next question is prepared in the background.
Repetitive battle and obscure exact-year questions have been substantially reduced.
Question-pack progress is now easier to understand at a glance.
The Achievements page is more compact and easier to scan.
You can now flag questions whose wording gives away the answer.

items.text: The timer now stops immediately when an answer is submitted.
Answer results return sooner by performing independent progress updates in parallel.
The next question is prepared in the background while you review the previous answer.
A prepared question's timer does not begin until you actually advance to it.
Answer-submission messages now use clearer language while the result is being verified.
Revised 196 questions based on player feedback, administrator review, and broader catalog analysis.
Processed all 57 action items from the latest production Question Quality export.
Replaced 21 repetitive battle-location questions with a broader mix of historical knowledge.
Reduced exact-year recall to 11 questions across the entire catalog, retaining important landmark dates.
Reclassified all 42 remaining General Knowledge questions into more meaningful topics and subtopics.
Improved accepted answers, wording, difficulty, explanations, answer modes, distractors, and supporting sources.
Replaced questions that did not belong in their topic-specific packs while preserving every pack's question count.
Players can now flag questions whose wording gives away the answer.
Feedback about rejected answers continues to include the answer the player submitted, helping reviewers identify missing aliases.
Reviewer displays summarize the different answers players have given and how often each answer occurred.
Question Packs now combine total, answered, and correct counts into one compact progress bar.
The Achievements page uses a denser layout so more milestones can be reviewed at once.
Authorized reviewers can begin reviewing a pack directly from the Question Packs page.
Added a dedicated Question Reviewer role without broader system-administrator privileges.
Added a pack-scoped Question Index showing every question, its classification, difficulty, answer mode, review status, player flags, attempts, correct answers, and accuracy.
Questions in the index link directly to their pack-scoped Question Quality review.
Question Quality now keeps the pack chooser out of the way after review begins.
Redundant aliases matching the canonical answer are hidden.
Player identities remain hidden from question reviewers while the relevant answer and aggregate evidence remain visible.
Review decisions and role changes now create their audit records in the same database transaction as the protected change.
Admin tools are organized under a role-aware Admin submenu.

releases.title: Smoother play and clearer progress
releases.summary: This update makes playing feel faster, simplifies getting started, clarifies progress, and incorporates Mindspan's first substantial round of beta question feedback.

highlights.text: Removed the initial assessment for a quicker start for new players.
Improved the clarity of proficiency and progress.
Reduced how often you need to log in.
Improved 35 questions based on player and reviewer feedback.

items.text: Play setup controls now wait until they are interactive, so a very fast first click is not silently ignored.
The timer now stops on screen as soon as you submit an answer, and answer controls lock while the result is finalized.
Normal network and server-processing time is no longer counted against an answer submitted on time.
Unsaved question-feedback details are saved automatically when you select Next question; if saving fails, the answer screen remains open.
New players now proceed directly from interest selection into Mindspan instead of completing a separate assessment.
Mindspan now keeps you signed in more reliably after closing and reopening your browser.
Display-name text entered immediately after onboarding appears is no longer lost while the interactive form finishes loading.
Historical assessment records, earned achievements, Insight, and mastery remain preserved.
Group leaderboards now describe supporting statistics as percentage correct and different questions answered.
The confusing overall proficiency percentage has been removed from group leaderboard summaries.
You can now report that a question is assigned to the wrong topic.
Feedback details are protected from being accidentally lost when advancing to the next question.
We reviewed and improved all 35 questions in the first production question-quality export, including player feedback that had not yet received an administrator review.
Corrections include topics, pack placement, accepted answers, answer modes, difficulty, wording, explanations, and supporting context.
No question was automatically removed based solely on player feedback.
Mindspan now keeps an in-app history of released changes and shows returning players a one-time summary after an upgrade.
The current Mindspan version is visible in the navigation and on the Account page.
Question Quality cards now show subtopics beside the topic, pack, and difficulty and identify typed-answer versus required-choice questions.
Question rejection is now labeled simply Reject and supports the X keyboard shortcut.
Question-quality exports and release tooling now support substantial catalog-review batches while preserving immutable question history.

releases.title: Question quality tools
releases.summary: Mindspan added faster ways for players and administrators to identify and correct question problems.

highlights.text: Players can report when an answer should have been accepted.
Administrators can review, filter, and export question-quality concerns.

items.text: Report that an answer you supplied should have been accepted.
Review and export player feedback through the Question Quality workspace.
The affected Thomas Jefferson question now accepts Jefferson as an answer.
